title: Campaign Launch Brief
summary: A structured briefing workflow that forces clarity before a campaign goes live across channels, content, and stakeholders.
whenToUseIt: Use this before any meaningful campaign launch, product push, seasonal promotion, or paid media burst.

steps.title: Define the business objective
steps.description: Write the exact result the campaign must produce: pipeline, signups, trials, bookings, retention, or revenue. Choose one primary objective.

steps.title: Clarify the audience and offer
steps.description: Document who the campaign is for, what pain or desire it speaks to, and what offer or next step the audience should take.

steps.title: Choose the channel mix and assets
steps.description: List the channels, assets, landing pages, emails, ads, and owners required for launch. Identify any dependency that could delay the campaign.

steps.title: Set the measurement plan
steps.description: Decide which KPIs matter, where they will be tracked, and what success and failure thresholds look like in the first 7-14 days.

steps.title: Run a pre-launch check
steps.description: Review messaging, links, tracking, forms, budget settings, and approvals. Do a dry run before the launch date.

expectedOutcome: A campaign everyone understands before launch, with clear ownership, required assets, and measurement discipline already locked in.
commonMistakes: Launching with no single business objective
Assuming creative readiness without checking landing pages, forms, and tracking
Using different messages across channels and weakening the campaign story
Waiting until after launch to decide how success will be measured
